name: algolia
description: Search, browse, and update records in Algolia search indices

envVars.name: ALGOLIA_API_KEY
envVars.description: Algolia API key (use the Search API key for read-only access, or a scoped key with addObject for writes)

envVars.name: ALGOLIA_APP_ID
envVars.description: Algolia application ID (10-character uppercase ID shown next to your API keys)

tools.name: List Indices
tools.description: List the indices in the Algolia application

host.description: Algolia host built from your application ID: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net", e.g. B1G2GM9NG0-dsn.algolia.net

page.description: Page of indices to fetch (0-based; omit to list all)

historicalSummary.omitted: index settings and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Search Index
tools.description: Run a search query against an index

host.description: Algolia host built from your application ID: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net", e.g. B1G2GM9NG0-dsn.algolia.net

indexName.description: Name of the index to search

filters.description: Filter expression, e.g. category:book AND price < 100

attributesToRetrieve.description: Record attributes to include in results, e.g. ["title","url"]

tools.name: Browse Index
tools.description: Browse all records in an index with cursor-based pagination

host.description: Algolia host built from your application ID: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net", e.g. B1G2GM9NG0-dsn.algolia.net

indexName.description: Name of the index to browse

query.description: Optional query to restrict the records browsed

cursor.description: Cursor from the previous browse response to fetch the next page

tools.name: Get Object
tools.description: Retrieve a single record from an index by objectID

host.description: Algolia host built from your application ID: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net", e.g. B1G2GM9NG0-dsn.algolia.net

objectID.description: Unique object ID of the record

tools.name: Save Objects
tools.description: Add or update records in an index via a batch operation

host.description: Algolia host built from your application ID: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net", e.g. B1G2GM9NG0-dsn.algolia.net

indexName.description: Name of the index to write to

requests.description: Batch operations, e.g. [{"action":"addObject","body":{"objectID":"1","title":"My record"}}]; actions include addObject, updateObject, partialUpdateObject, deleteObject

prompts.title: Search an index
prompts.prompt: Search my Algolia index for a query I provide and summarize the top hits.

prompts.title: Index overview
prompts.prompt: List my Algolia indices with their record counts and last update times.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Algolia API Setup

steps.title: Create an Algolia application
steps.description: Sign up at https://dashboard.algolia.com (the free Build plan works for testing) and create or select an application.

steps.title: Copy your credentials
steps.description: In Settings → API Keys (https://dashboard.algolia.com/account/api-keys/all), copy the Application ID into ALGOLIA_APP_ID and an API key into ALGOLIA_API_KEY. Use the Search API key for read-only use, or create a scoped key with search, browse, and addObject ACLs for writes — avoid the Admin API key.

steps.title: Note your host
steps.description: Tools take a 'host' parameter: pass "<ALGOLIA_APP_ID>-dsn.algolia.net" (your application ID followed by -dsn.algolia.net).

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Indices tool. A 403 usually means the API key lacks the required ACL or the host does not match your application ID.

notes: Algolia authenticates with two headers: X-Algolia-API-Key and X-Algolia-Application-Id — both env vars are required
Browse requires an API key with the 'browse' ACL; Save Objects requires 'addObject'
The Search API key is safe to expose in frontends, but scoped/write keys must stay server-side
